message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Population Policy Acceptance Study (PPAS), 1988 – Federal Republic of Germany (West Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11619","justification":"PPAS includes detailed items on fertility intentions, family planning, and attitudes toward reproductive behavior for West Germany in 1988, fitting reproductive health and the 1980s period."},{"title":"European Fertility and Family Survey (FFS), Germany 1988","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA2862","justification":"This survey contains retrospective fertility histories, contraceptive use, and partnership trajectories for Germany with reference to the late 1970s and 1980s, directly related to reproductive health."},{"title":"Fertility and Family Survey, German Democratic Republic (GDR), 1988","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA2861","justification":"Covers women’s fertility histories, contraception, and family formation in the GDR around 1988, providing reproductive health–relevant data for East Germany in the 1980s."},{"title":"Maternity and Family Planning in the Federal Republic of Germany 1984","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA1273","justification":"Survey focuses explicitly on maternity, contraception, and family planning among women in West Germany in 1984, directly addressing reproductive health."},{"title":"Maternity and Family Planning in the Federal Republic of Germany 1980","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA1031","justification":"Examines birth histories, contraceptive use, and family planning among women in West Germany at the beginning of the 1980s, central to reproductive health questions."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 1986","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA1610","justification":"While broader in scope, the 1986 ALLBUS includes modules on family, gender roles, and partly on fertility attitudes, which are relevant to reproductive health in mid‑1980s West Germany."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 1988","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA1710","justification":"Provides attitudinal data on family, partnership, gender roles, and some fertility-related questions for West Germany at the end of the 1980s."},{"title":"Family and Fertility Survey, Federal Republic of Germany, 1984","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA1234","justification":"Includes parity, timing of births, contraceptive behavior, and partnership histories, directly relating to reproductive health in 1980s West Germany."},{"title":"Health Survey: Women’s Health and Contraception in the Federal Republic of Germany 1987","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA1735","justification":"Specialized health survey focusing on women’s reproductive health, including contraception and gynecological care, for West Germany in 1987."},{"title":"Microcensus Sample 1987 – Fertility and Family Supplement (Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA1987","justification":"Large-scale official sample with a fertility and family module, providing births, number of children, and some reproductive behavior indicators for Germany in 1987."}]}
